gpxTracks.name: Ala-Archa - Ak-Sai Racek Hut and Glacier

locations.name: Min-Kush
locations.description: Soviet uranium ghost town. Population dropped from 20k to 2k. Decaying apartment blocks, massive Lenin statue.

locations.name: Sharkyratma Waterfall
locations.description: Waterfall near the shores of Song-Kol. Less visited than main lake viewpoints.

locations.name: Moldo-Ashuu Pass
locations.description: 3,346m pass on the road to Song-Kol. Dramatic alpine scenery, possible snow in April.

locations.name: Burana Tower
locations.description: 11th century minaret, 25m tall. Balbals collection nearby. Small museum. 150 som entry.

locations.name: Kochkor Lenin Statue
locations.description: Silver-painted Lenin in front of regional government

locations.name: Kochkor Petroglyphs
locations.description: Ancient rock carvings near Kochkor

locations.name: Barskoon
locations.description: Village with waterfalls and Gagarin monument. Soviet lorry on plinth nearby.

locations.name: Jeti-Oguz Valley
locations.description: Iconic red rock fins rising from green hills. 'Broken Heart' rock across the road.

locations.name: Jeti-Oguz Resort
locations.description: Soviet-era resort with healing geothermal springs at 2200m.

locations.name: Jeti-Oguz Memorial
locations.description: Memorial in Jeti-Oguz town.

locations.name: Skazka Canyon
locations.description: Martian landscape on Issyk-Kul's south shore. Red sandstone formations. Best at sunset. 50 som entry.

locations.name: Tastar-Ata
locations.description: Sacred pilgrimage site and hiking area. Petroglyphs and spiritual significance.

locations.name: Arabel Pass Viewpoint
locations.description: High-altitude viewpoint at ~3,900m on the road to Kumtor mine. Dramatic mountain views.

locations.name: Abandoned Young Pioneer Camp
locations.description: Massive Soviet summer camp left to rot since 1991. Near Bel-Tam on south shore.

locations.name: Aurora Sanatorium
locations.description: Ship-shaped brutalist sanatorium built 1979 for Communist Party elite. Still operating.

locations.name: Aalam Ordo
locations.description: Cultural center near Cholpon-Ata. Soviet-meets-Kyrgyz architecture, sculptures.

locations.name: Issyk-Ata Sanatorium
locations.description: Near Bishkek. One of Central Asia's oldest. WW2 soldier treatment center.

locations.name: Cholpon-Ata Petroglyphs
locations.description: 42 hectares of Bronze Age rock carvings. 5,000+ stones. Hunting scenes, sun worship.

locations.name: Dungan Mosque
locations.description: Chinese Buddhist temple that's actually a mosque. Built 1907-1910 entirely of wood with zero nails.

locations.name: Ashlan-fu Alley
locations.description: Bogu Bazaar. Multiple stalls serving Karakol's signature dish. 600 bowls/day at 30 som.

locations.name: Altyn-Arashan Hot Springs
locations.description: Natural hot springs at end of 15km trek. Soviet-era huts, alpine meadows.

locations.name: Karakol Town
locations.description: Melting pot - Kyrgyz, Dungan, Tatar, Uyghur, Russian. Best food scene in the country.

locations.name: Jyrgalan Valley
locations.description: 60km from Karakol. Former coal mining village turned adventure tourism hub.

locations.name: State History Museum
locations.description: Massive Soviet-era museum on Ala-Too Square. Kyrgyz history from nomads to independence.

locations.name: Great Emptiness Museum
locations.description: Underground Soviet bunker turned art space. Nuclear shelter vibes meets contemporary installations.

locations.name: Osh Bazaar
locations.description: Massive chaotic market. Food, clothes, everything. The real Bishkek experience.

locations.name: Panfilov Park
locations.description: Central park with Soviet monuments. Good for a walk.

locations.name: Kyrgyz State Circus
locations.description: Soviet-era circus building. Shows still run.

locations.name: Victory Square
locations.description: Soviet war memorial and eternal flame.

locations.name: Kyrgyz National Museum of Fine Arts
locations.description: Gapar Aitiev museum. Soviet and Kyrgyz art collection.

locations.name: Sulaiman Mountain Museum
locations.description: UNESCO site. Sacred mountain in the middle of Osh city. Museum built into the rock.

locations.name: Arslanbob
locations.description: Village in the world's largest walnut forest. Waterfalls, horse treks, CBT homestays.

locations.name: Saimaluu Tash
locations.description: One of the largest petroglyph sites in Central Asia. 10,000+ Bronze Age carvings at 3,000m.

locations.name: Tash Rabat
locations.description: 15th century stone caravanserai at 3,200m. Remarkably intact Silk Road waystation.

locations.name: Kol-Suu
locations.description: Stunning turquoise lake near Chinese border. Remote, requires permit and 4x4.

locations.name: Enilchek
locations.description: Former mining town, gateway to South Inylchek Glacier and Khan Tengri base camp.

locations.name: Khrebet Sarydzhaz
locations.description: Mountain range on the Chinese border. Includes Khan Tengri (6,995m).

locations.name: Yrdyk
locations.description: Remote area east of Karakol. Less visited, rugged landscapes.
